title: chore: Enable codeql action
body:
This action runs GitHub's industry-leading semantic code analysis engine, CodeQL, against a repository's source code to find security vulnerabilities.

https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/code-scanning/automatically-scanning-your-code-for-vulnerabilities-and-errors/about-code-scanning-with-codeql

https://github.com/ossf/scorecard/blob/main/docs/checks.md#sast

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *naveensrinivasan* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **rhatdan** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @rhatdan` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
not sure I love a security analysis tool that reports publicly. @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

> not sure I love a security analysis tool that reports publicly. @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

I understand. But anyone can fork and run this. So this report is technically available to everyone!

I do not see the value of this. It is complaining about the use paths that the user can influence? That just doesn't make any sense to me. We expect paths from users and that is a completely valid use case.

If want this we could enable this as linter in golangci-lint and do not need an extra github actions job for this.
As is, I also suspect this is non-blocking - the only blocking job, AFAIK, is Cirrus "Total Success"
I am for adding to linter, if we can tamp it down to allow users to input paths.
